medications.indication_rationale: First-line topical antifungal for oral thrush; source prescribed nystatin 100,000 units/mL, 4 mL swish-and-swallow four times daily for 7–14 days.
medications.dose_route_frequency: 4 mL swished for ~2 minutes then swallowed, four times daily for 7–14 days (as per source note)

medications.drug_name: topical antifungal for angular cheilitis (e.g. miconazole)

medications.indication_rationale: Analgesia to enable hydration; source advised 1000mg up to four times daily.
medications.dose_route_frequency: 1000mg orally up to four times daily (max 4g/day) (as per source note)

medications.indication_rationale: This is a fungal (not bacterial) overgrowth precipitated by antibiotics — more antibiotics would worsen it.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Do not eat/drink for ~30 minutes after the nystatin dose; use cool bland fluids and a straw with analgesia timed before drinking; use a soft toothbrush and replace it after the infection clears.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Maximises antifungal contact time and maintains hydration; a new toothbrush prevents re-inoculation.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Avoid acidic/spicy/rough foods; prefer cool bland foods.

patient_education_points.point: This is oral thrush — a yeast overgrowth after your antibiotics upset the normal balance in your mouth. The antifungal mouthwash clears it over 7–10 days; keep going 48 hours after it looks clear to prevent relapse.
Don't eat or drink for 30 minutes after the dose so it can work. Keep drinking cool bland fluids (use pain relief first), and get a new toothbrush once it clears.

Go to ED if you get deep chest pain when swallowing or a sense of food sticking, become unable to drink at all, stop passing urine, or feel very lightheaded. See your GP if this keeps coming back or has no clear cause (to check for things like diabetes).

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Clearance and normalised intake; investigate predisposing factors if recurrent/unexplained.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Deep odynophagia/food sticking, unable to rehydrate — ED.

must_include_items: Topical antifungal (nystatin/miconazole) with correct swish-and-swallow / contact-time technique
acceptable_alternatives: Miconazole oral gel as alternative topical antifungal; treat angular cheilitis
errors_of_omission: Not considering underlying immunosuppression when unexplained
errors_of_commission: Systemic antifungal as first-line for simple oral thrush without oesophageal involvement (usually unnecessary)
Ignoring the dehydration risk in a patient living alone
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires topical antifungal with correct technique, an oesophageal-candidiasis safety-net, and a dehydration safety-net.
